titleStmt.title: SII 28.257: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 257. (A.R. No. 411 of 1913.)

summary.p: Kāmēśvara temple, central shrine, north wall.

p.hi: ••• ••••• Śaka 1430, Vibhava, Kuṁbha śu. 10, Sunday, Āyilyam Irregular.

summary.p: Records that Eṟamāñchi Tulukkaṇa-nāyakkar, on finding that festival for the God Tirukkāmīśvaram-uḍaiya-ṉāyaṉār of Āṟagaḷ-ūr in Magadai-maṇḍalam was not being celebrated from the time of the Pāṇḍyas till that date, made a chariot (car) for that God and arranged for the celebration of the serial-festival in the month of Māśi (with the chariot festival on the day of Āyilyam) and the bathing of the God (tiru-mañjaṉam) on the day of Magham. During that festival, for beating of the drum (paṟai) one Paṟaiyan, son of Perumāḷ of Tāmaka •• nallūr was engaged. For him (the kaikkōḷan) the God's secretary Ādichaṇḍēśvaranāyaṉār is stated to have been symbolically represented to, by Mudaliyār Chaṉṉakamala-ṉāyaṉār Ādichaṇḍēśvara-nāyanār, Tārum (?) and Māhēśvarar, who had assembled at Āṟai-ṉāyaka-maṇḍapam for the grant of wet and dry land and a house-site in Āṟagaḷūr and also cooked rice (chōṟu) prepared out of 2 nāḻi of rice to be doled out to him in the temple. This privilege (chuvantiram) was to be enjoyed by the father of Perumāḷ and his lineage (vargattār) till eternity.

This mention of the father of the beneficiary as the first one (or the associate one) to enjoy the benefit of the grant suggests that Perumāḷ might have died or in some way got (incapacitated). In line 7 the word starting with the letters uti and ending with ṭi the intervening letters of which have been built in might have read udirappaṭṭi and the benefaction might have been made to honour him in recognition of some service, he had rendered. It is possible that the surname of kaikkōḷan indicated that Paṟaiyaṉs [family was hereditarily engaged as troopers in the regular army of the king and the beating of drum was an occupation taken up by paṟaiyaṉ either in between military engagements or on becoming too old to be an effective soldier].

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . cakāttam 1000 4 100 3 10 Itan mel cellā ninṟa vipava caṅvaṟcarattu kumpa nāyaṟṟu pūrvvapakṣattu tecamiyum
Ātittavāramum Āyiliyattu nāḷ makataimaṇṭalam Āṟakaḻ Ūr nāyiṉār tirukkāmicuramuṭaiya ṉāyaṉār pāṇṭiyar ṉāḷ mutal Innāḷ varaikkum
tirunāḷum naṭavātirukkaiyil cuvāmi nāḷokaṇṭan vaṅki nārāyaṇan maṟaipukkaārkāvalan vallāḷa Irāya bhayaṅkaran cāṭikkāṟar kaṇṭa
n śrīmatu Eṟamāñci tulukkaṇanāyakkar nayaṉār tirukkāmicuramuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟkku terum camaittu tirunāḷum mācittirunāḷāka Āyiliyattile terum
makattile tirumañcanamum Āka kaṭṭaḷai paṇṇi tirunāḷ naṭakkayil tāmaka nallūril kaikkoḷan perumāḷ makaṉ paṟaiyan paṟai Eṭuttu mutaliyār caṉṉakamala
ṉāyaṉār Āticaṇṭecura nāyanārum tārumum mākecurarum Āka Āṟai ṉāyaka maṇṭapattil niṟaivaṟa niṟaintu kuṟaivaṟa kūṭi Iruntu Āticaṇṭecuraṉāyaṉār
tiruvāy malarntaruḷinapaṭi Inta paṟaiyaṉukku Utit ṭi Āka Āṟakaḻūr naṉcey nilattil kāḷi Ammai Uṭaippukku meṟku tiyākaṇa nallūr vaya
lukku kiḻakku naṉcey rūpāy 2 100 5 10 Oraṇai Er naṭatta maṭṭu puṉcey mā nipamum Orumaṉaiyum Oru kkum māṉiyamum tirumañcaṉa vaḻikku teṟku Iraṇ
ṭu Oru toṭṭamum koyilile niṟṟam Irunāḻi Aruciyil coṟum maṉaiyāvatu cannati teruvil vaṭaciṟakil Oru maṉaiyum kuṭuttaruḷi
Āticaṇṭecuraṉāyaṉār tiruvāy malarntaruḷinapaṭiyāle Inta cuvantiram Uṇṭānatu Ivan takappaṉ perumāḷum Ivan vaṟkattārum cantiṟātit
Aṉupavittu koḷḷakaṭavarkaḷākavum Ivai tiruñāna campanta paṇṭitar Eḻuttu Ivai palamutal kaṅkāṇi periya nayiṉār Eḻuttu §
Digital edition of SII 28.257 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
